title: Retry Safety, Replayability, Backoff, Jitter, and Retry Budgets · Browser and Node Client Systems · Compiler Case: Backoff Jitter
summary: A high-traffic web SDK retries selected reads under 429 and 503 without replaying one-shot streams or synchronizing every browser tab. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts backoff jitter increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch bounded retry and replay decision engine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply capped exponential backoff with an explicit jitter strategy and deterministic test clock. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Prove request bodies and credential state can be reconstructed before replaying an attempt. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Predict Backoff Jitter · Theory Backoff Jitter
steps.instruction:
theory case — TypeScript Fetch evidence model sees one reset, Retry-After:6, then 503 while a nested helper also retries. Prove method and body replayability, cap attempts and elapsed budget, apply deterministic jitter, and prevent duplicate or amplified work. Competency probe: Apply capped exponential backoff with an explicit jitter strategy and deterministic test clock. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
